projects.name: Fest Forum
projects.description: A bilingual digital platform for Poland's festival industry, built for the Kraków Festival Office and the National Centre for Culture. It pairs a public festival site with a gated membership community where organizers register, build profiles, and publish their own articles through a moderated submission flow. I architected the frontend against two backends at once — a headless WordPress driving the editorial pages, and a custom API handling accounts, authentication, membership, and submissions. Core work includes a multi-step application builder, an event program with capacity-limited registration, edition-driven theming, and a fully WCAG-compliant UI. The project is ongoing.

projects.name: BUILD/LOG
projects.description: A construction site-management platform that pulls a job site's tasks, defects, and photo documentation into one workspace — replacing scattered emails, chat threads, and paper. Built as part of a team, I integrated a custom PHP backend with a React and React Query frontend, building the kanban-style task tracking, filterable tables, multi-step forms, and detailed item views that the role-specific dashboards — site managers, contractors, inspectors — all run on.

projects.name: The Makery
projects.description: A multilingual admin dashboard and marketplace for an artisan workshop platform with hundreds of existing users. I was responsible for the transition from an aging, slow admin interface to a modern tech stack in Next.js — rebuilding data management views, user flows, and the core marketplace functionality while keeping the platform operational for its existing user base throughout the migration.

projects.name: BHT Startup Hub
projects.description: The website for Berlin University of Technology's startup incubator — a lean accelerator that has supported founders with coaching, coworking space, and equity-free scholarships. I built the custom frontend integrated with a WordPress backend.

projects.name: Navarra Lab
projects.description: The company portfolio website for Navarra Lab — the design studio and software house where I work. This site is also the foundation that my personal portfolio is built on.

projects.name: Ultramaryna
projects.description: A cultural events discovery platform, operating as a free guide to the Silesia region's cultural life. The platform lets users browse events across theater, music, visual arts, and film. I built the frontend for the digital platform, including the event browsing experience, newsletter integration, and the magazine content section.

projects.name: Murat Yumak
projects.description: A bilingual e-commerce platform for a Berlin-based curator of vintage streetwear classics from the 80s to the present. The site operates like a museum meets boutique — each item comes with cultural context and provenance, and objects remain viewable even after being sold. I built the bilingual Shopify-integrated frontend with advanced filtering, along with the archival browsing experience that sets the shop apart from typical resale platforms.

projects.name: Eggplant Notes
projects.description:
Built as part of the 10xDevs program — an exploration of AI native software engineering across the full development workflow. 
The result is a feature-rich spaced-repetition learning app, built from scratch with AI in just a few weeks. Notes are grouped into subjects and rendered with syntax-highlighted code, then turned into recall cards scheduled by the FSRS algorithm. Users connect their own OpenRouter key to generate notes and cards with AI (hundreds of models to choose from), and a token-authenticated HTTP API lets coding agents talk to the app directly.

projects.name: Chaos Kitchen
projects.description:
A high-end personal-brand site with a full ebook store, delivered as a single-page scrolling experience. Everything here is mine — the design concept and visual identity, the cart and store flow, and the full stack, from the Payload CMS backend to the animated frontend.
This is where I started experimenting with AI-powered design and animations. Only a few months ago, a page like this would have taken months and a design team — this time I built it all on my own.

projects.name: Wykonczymy
projects.description:
It started when the owner of a growing company came to me with a familiar problem: the business had outgrown its tools. Cash flow, expenses, every investment — all of it lived in a tangle of Google Sheets that was impossible to keep in sync and a real bottleneck day to day. We wanted to try to use AI assisted coding to design and built a small app to take the pressure off, and it worked so well that we decided to move the entire operation off Sheets and into it.
Today it's a full operations platform handling hundreds of transactions a month: multi-register cash management on a dual-ledger model (real cash flow vs. per-investment P&L — margins, balances, labor and material costs), team-wide project management with role-based access, and an in-app cost-estimate editor with work stages, a shared catalogue, and live totals. We're now in the middle of the full migration — keeping the existing Sheets running while everything moves into the app. I am building it end to end and the work is ongoing.

projects.name: Moodbox
projects.description:
A material-sampling platform for interior designers, built end-to-end. When the client came to me, all that existed was the idea and a single-page design she had mocked up in Canva and wanted implemented — I took it from there to a full platform: a Shopify-integrated storefront backed by a custom Payload CMS on Postgres.
Shopify runs the storefront, but the whole point of the platform is capturing structured feedback from designers on the samples they receive. That backend powers a post-purchase feedback loop: scheduled-email queue that, seven days after a box ships, emails each customer a tokenized link to a multi-step brand survey. The survey gathers per-brand ratings and reasons through conditional, cross-validated steps, then issues a one-off discount code in return.

projects.name: Olej.to
projects.description: A lifestyle blog platform about the struggles and joys of simple life — blending humor, travel stories and personal reflection. I implemented a WordPress backend for content management paired with a custom Svelte frontend, delivering a clean, minimal reading experience that prioritizes typography and quick scanning across categories like humor, serious essays, and personal stories. My first full stack solo work and first encounter with Svelte.
